element_choice.justification:
Every element is the best semantic match:
for product cards,

aria_discipline.justification: Mostly disciplined: <nav> elements are labelled, <section> and <aside> landmarks are labelled, aria-current is used correctly, aria-labelledby on 'Add to cart' buttons is a legitimate technique. One redundancy: role="search" on the <form> inside the <search> element is unnecessary because <search> already exposes the search landmark role.

heading_hierarchy.justification: Single h1 ('Running Shoes'), h2 for section headings (filter sidebar, results count, footer sections), h3 for product names — no skipped levels. A comment explicitly notes the h3 assumption and instructs reusers to adjust the level if the component is lifted to a different context.

landmark_structure.justification: All expected landmarks are present and correctly placed: <header>, <nav aria-label="Main">, <search>, <aside>, <main id="main-content">, <footer>. Three skip links (content, search, filters) are the first focusable elements and appropriate for this page's complexity. <main> correctly excludes the site header, search bar, and footer. <section> in footer has aria-labelledby.

form_semantics.justification: All inputs have associated <label> elements. Each filter group is wrapped in a <fieldset> with a <legend>. Correct input types used (type="search", type="checkbox"). The in-stock toggle uses a native checkbox. No placeholder-only labels.

content_realism.justification: Six realistic product names (Nike Air Zoom Pegasus 41, Adidas Ultraboost 24, Brooks Ghost 16, ASICS Gel-Kayano 31, Hoka Clifton 9, New Balance Fresh Foam X 1080v13) with varied, plausible prices and specific product descriptions. No 'Product 1' or lorem ipsum anywhere.

list_semantics.justification: Navigation items are in <ul> within <nav>. Product cards are in a <ul> (unordered, as sequence is not meaningful). Footer nav links are in <ul>. Correct list types throughout.

expectations_met: <main> contains only the primary page content — site header, footer, and primary nav are outside it
Skip navigation links are the first focusable elements — 'Skip to main content', 'Skip to search', and 'Skip to filters' all present
Navigation uses <nav> with a label that does NOT include the word 'navigation'
Filter sidebar uses <form> with <fieldset>/<legend> for each filter group
Heading hierarchy flows logically: h1 for page, h2 for sections, h3 for product names
Product card heading level is documented via inline comment noting it should be adjusted when reused at different nesting levels
Buttons are <button> elements, not <div> or <a> with click handlers
'Add to cart' buttons have accessible names via aria-labelledby referencing both the button and the product name element
Price is not marked up as a heading — uses
Filter checkboxes use proper <label> elements, not placeholders
The 'in stock only' toggle uses a native checkbox
expectations_missed: No ARIA attributes used where native HTML provides the same semantics — role="search" on the <form> inside the <search> element is redundant since <search> already provides the search landmark role
additional_observations: tabindex="-1" on <main> is correctly applied to allow skip links to programmatically focus it
<address> is used correctly for business contact information in the footer
aria-current="page" is used correctly on the active navigation link
Three skip links provided (content, search, filters) is thorough and appropriate for this page's complexity with both a search bar and a filter sidebar
